titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 27 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.
In this letter Spender is writing to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution.
Spender complains of the disturbance to his sleep caused by a painful wisdom tooth and the increasingly noisy front. He despairs at the lack of purpose or consistency in the fighting, and states his growing support for a 'benevolent autocracy'.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
27 June My pr Only a scrap to
accompany my note to Lil
which you need not pass on
if you think it best but not to
do so. With an old wisdom
tooth & a head, & 3 AM
bed this bw is not quite
his best, tho' he got a
new packet of mitcum
- sheets - which did him
good. I'm very much afraid
of your being over worked &
wish some of the others
could come & help you. I
think you must try & get
some regular worker now
& perhaps some of Miss Scrum
might do failing others . I
will gladly pay expenses. A great deal of noise
today which will I suppose
continue for some time,
but one is a little disappointed
by the lack of
steady purpose, & the changes
which perpetually occur.
I believe more & more in
a benevolent autocracy ! I am very busy &
shall be for some days
so you will get nothing
but scraps. Your very own bw
WBSpender
